name: DNS SOA Configuration Drift and TTL Misconfiguration
description: Detects SOA record misconfigurations that can lead to zone drift (primary/secondary mismatch) or zone thrash (excessive zone transfers causing DoS). Per NIST SP 800-81r3 Section 3.2.2, SOA Refresh should be 1200s-43200s, Retry should be a fraction of Refresh, and TTL values should be 1800s-86400s. TTL of zero is explicitly prohibited, and values below 30s are discouraged for DNSSEC-signed records.
author: AC3 ScanForge

values: TTL below 30 seconds on DNSSEC-signed record
SOA serial mismatch between primary and secondary

remediation: Set SOA Refresh to between 1200 seconds (20 minutes) and 43200 seconds (12 hours). Set SOA Retry to a fraction of the Refresh interval. Ensure TTL values are between 1800 seconds (30 minutes) and 86400 seconds (1 day). Never use TTL of zero. For DNSSEC-signed records, ensure TTL is at least 30 seconds. Monitor SOA serial numbers across primary and secondary servers to detect zone drift. Verify primary/secondary synchronization after zone changes.

intelligence.dfirRelevance: SOA misconfigurations can cause DNS service degradation or outages. Zone thrash from overly aggressive Refresh/Retry values can be exploited as an amplification vector for DoS attacks against DNS infrastructure.
